name: Mocha-Almond Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Spread almonds on a baking sheet and bake for 12 to 14 minutes, or until lightly toasted. Set aside.
3. Stir together flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a bowl.
4. Whisk together eggs, egg whites, and vanilla extract; add to the dry ingredients and mix just until smooth.
5. In a small bowl, combine cocoa, instant coffee, and 2 tbsp water.
6. Divide dough into two halves. Add the cocoa mixture and melted chocolate to one half; mix until incorporated.
7. Stir in almond extract and toasted almonds for the other half.
8. Place half of the almond dough on a well-floured work surface. Pat into a 4x8-inch rectangle.
9. Spread half of the chocolate dough over the almond dough.
10. Roll up the mixture into a cylinder, then roll it back and forth to form a 14-inch log, about 1 1/2 inches thick.
11. Repeat with the remaining dough.
12. Place logs on a prepared baking sheet. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, or until firm to the touch.
13. Transfer logs to a rack to cool.
14. Reduce oven temperature to 300°F.
15. Cut logs diagonally into 1/2-inch thick slices.
16. Stand slices upright on the baking sheet and bake for an additional 40 minutes.
17. Let cool before storing.
Makes about 4 dozen biscotti.
